response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a technically accomplished engineer with a strong craftsmanship background, but his profile is a poor fit for a JVM/IDE Tooling role. Java appears only in minor, dated contexts (robotic arm work at Altom, 2016–2018; some C/C++ for NI LabWindows/CVI), and there is no evidence of Kotlin, Spring, Hibernate, Quarkus, or any JVM-deep expertise. His primary technical identity is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. The NI LabWindows contribution is the one adjacent data point — IDE tooling work — but it is over a decade old and in C, not the JVM stack the role explicitly requires.
strengths: Consistent software craftsmanship discipline (TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD) applied across multiple senior roles
Has built developer-facing tooling (test runners, assertion libraries, MCP code generators), demonstrating genuine interest in developer experience
Experience contributing to NI LabWindows/CVI, a real IDE product, showing at least one prior exposure to the domain
concerns: No demonstrable Java or Kotlin depth; Java is listed in skills but appears only in peripheral, dated use cases
Zero experience with Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise framework — the core technical focus of the role
Primary language stack (TypeScript/Node.js, DLang) is fundamentally misaligned with the JVM-centric requirements

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise framework experience
Developer tooling and IDE-adjacent work history
